Before You Read

✦

Everything is
taken care of.

I have put this together so that you have a complete and clear picture of Serah's entire journey — where she will be, who she will be with, how she gets from place to place, and how she comes home safely. From the moment she boards her flight in Nairobi to the moment she walks back through the door, every leg of this trip has been thought through, every detail confirmed, and every connection covered. She will not be navigating anything alone. Family and close friends are with her at every step. This is not just a trip — it is a welcome.

— Prepared by Neeresh

Day One  ·  Friday 22 May 2026

Nairobi to Delhi
The Arrival

With

Smriti joins at Mumbai
Neeresh & Rajan at Delhi arrivals

3:30 AM — Nairobi Time

Wake Up & Head to Jomo Kenyatta Airport

Check-in reference is T9CTRM. Leave home by 4:00 AM to reach the airport by 4:45 AM — comfortable time for international check-in and security.

06:40 — Nairobi NBO, Terminal 1C

Flight 6E 1854 Departs — Nairobi to Mumbai

IndiGo · 6h 20m · Economy · 1 piece of luggage (max 25 kg). A long comfortable flight — arrive rested in Mumbai.

15:30 IST — Mumbai BOM, Terminal 2

Lands Mumbai — Smriti Is Waiting

About 3.5 hours at the terminal — enough time to eat, freshen up, and check in for the Delhi flight. Smriti Priyadarshi is at Terminal 2 waiting. From this moment, Serah is with family.

19:30 IST — Mumbai T2 to Delhi T2

Flight to Delhi, With Smriti

Domestic, approximately 2 hours. Pulkit also arrives Delhi the same evening on a separate flight — the whole group converges tonight.

~21:30 IST — Delhi IGI Airport, Terminal 2 Arrivals

Received at Delhi Airport

Neeresh and Rajan are both waiting at arrivals. Serah does not have to figure out anything on her own — the moment she walks out, familiar faces are right there.

🏠

Drive to Athiti Home Stay — Greater Noida

~45–55 min · Self check-in · Full kitchen · Private garden · 5-night home base

~22:30

Day Two  ·  Saturday 23 May 2026

Old Delhi
Heritage & Welcome

With

Neeresh · Smriti · Rajan · Pulkit
Harit Kumar joins for dinner

9:00 AM — Athiti Home Stay

Breakfast at the Homestay

No early rush on the first full day. The kitchen is fully stocked. A proper home breakfast before heading out — no pressure, no hurry.

10:30 AM — Old Delhi

Jama Masjid and Chandni Chowk

One of the oldest and most impressive mosques in Asia, followed by the electric spice markets and street food lanes of Chandni Chowk. Serah's first real, proper taste of what Delhi actually feels like.

Old Delhi · Heritage Site · Built 1656

Jama Masjid & Chandni Chowk

Built by the same Emperor who built the Taj Mahal. One of the largest mosques in India. The lanes of Chandni Chowk stretch from its steps — spices, silks, street food, and centuries of history all in walking distance.

1:30 PM — Old Delhi

Lunch at Karim's

◆

Lunch · Old Delhi Classic

Karim's, near Jama Masjid

Over a hundred years old. Famous mughlai food — mutton korma, seekh kebabs, nihari. A must on any first visit to Old Delhi.

3:30 PM — Central Delhi

Red Fort — Lal Qila

The great Mughal fort, built in 1639. UNESCO World Heritage site. Best in the late afternoon when the red sandstone glows.

Central Delhi · UNESCO Heritage · Built 1639

Red Fort — Lal Qila

The grand Mughal fort that served as the main residence of the emperors for nearly 200 years. Late afternoon light makes the red sandstone glow beautifully.

7:30 PM — Delhi

Welcome Dinner — The Full Group

The one evening Harit Kumar joins. A proper group dinner to welcome Serah — good food, good company, everyone meeting properly for the first time. A moment worth having.

“The evening the whole group gathers — old faces and new, family and friends, all around one table.”

Day Three  ·  Sunday 24 May 2026

Agra Day Trip
The Taj at Sunrise

With

Neeresh · Smriti · Rajan · Pulkit

5:00 AM — Early Start

Depart Greater Noida for Agra

Gatimaan Express departs Hazrat Nizamuddin at 06:10, arrives Agra by 07:45 — fastest and most comfortable, 1st class AC recommended. Or hired cab, about 2.5 hours. Sunday morning traffic is much lighter.

7:45 AM — Agra

Taj Mahal — Sunrise Visit

Going early means golden light and thin crowds. One of those places that is genuinely hard to put into words until you are standing in front of it.

Agra · UNESCO World Heritage · Built 1631–1648

The Taj Mahal

Built by Emperor Shah Jahan for his wife Mumtaz Mahal. At sunrise, the white marble shifts through shades of rose and gold. The reflection pools are still, the air is quiet, and it is simply breathtaking.

11:00 AM — Agra

Agra Fort

A short drive from the Taj. The great red sandstone fort has a direct view of the Taj Mahal from across the Yamuna river — a completely different perspective worth seeing.

Agra · UNESCO World Heritage

Agra Fort

A massive red sandstone fort with a direct view across the Yamuna river to the Taj Mahal — a view that defined one of history's great love stories.

1:00 PM — Agra

Lunch in Agra

◆

Lunch · Agra

Pind Balluchi or Dasaprakash

Both well-regarded near the Taj area. A proper sit-down meal before the journey back.

3:30 PM — Agra to Greater Noida

Return to the Homestay

Train or cab back. Home by around 7:30 PM. A quiet evening at the homestay — reflect on a remarkable day.

Day Four  ·  Monday 25 May 2026

Family & the City
Renu's Home & Connaught Place

With

Neeresh · Smriti · Rajan · Pulkit
+ Renu & Advita in the morning

11:00 AM — Delhi

Visit to Renu Aunty's Home

A proper home visit with Neeresh's aunt Renu and her daughter Advita. Good food, lots of conversation, and a chance for Serah to meet that side of the family in a comfortable, relaxed setting.

12:30 PM

Home-Cooked Lunch at Renu's

●

Lunch · Home Cooked

At Renu Aunty's

There will definitely be a proper home-cooked spread. That is just how it goes when you visit family in India. No restaurant can match it.

4:30 PM — New Delhi

Connaught Place — Shopping and Evening Out

Delhi's grand colonnaded shopping district. Branded stores, Indian boutiques, and Janpath Market nearby — great for ethnic wear, handicrafts, and gifts to take back to Nairobi.

Central Delhi · Shopping District · 1930s

Connaught Place

A grand circular colonnaded market with everything from international brands to Indian boutiques. Janpath street market nearby is excellent for textiles, jewellery, and souvenirs at great prices.

7:30 PM — Connaught Place

Dinner at Farzi Cafe or Rajdhani

◆

Dinner · Connaught Place

Farzi Cafe or Rajdhani

Farzi Cafe — modern Indian cuisine, great energy, right at CP. Rajdhani — a wonderful traditional thali for something more classic.

Day Five  ·  Tuesday 26 May 2026

South Delhi
Serah & Neeresh

Just the Two of Them

Neeresh & Serah

10:00 AM — South Delhi

Humayun's Tomb and Lodhi Gardens

One of Delhi's most beautiful and underrated places. Humayun's Tomb is the architectural forerunner of the Taj Mahal — built 60 years before it, just as breathtaking, and far quieter. Lodhi Gardens next door is a peaceful morning walk through ancient tombs and lush greenery.

South Delhi · UNESCO Heritage · Built 1570

Humayun's Tomb

The inspiration for the Taj Mahal. The symmetry, the double dome, the red sandstone and white marble — all of it a full generation ahead of its time. Quieter than the Taj, and deeply beautiful for it.

1:00 PM — South Delhi

Lunch

Will pick a spot on the day depending on what Serah feels like. South Delhi has excellent options at every level.

3:00 PM — South Delhi

Qutub Minar

The tallest brick minaret in the world, surrounded by ancient Mughal ruins. A very different kind of history from the Taj or the Red Fort — older, quieter, stranger.

South Delhi · UNESCO Heritage · Built 1193

Qutub Minar

A 73-metre minaret — the tallest brick minaret on earth. The surrounding complex contains some of the earliest Islamic architecture in India. Quiet, layered, and historically extraordinary.

6:00 PM — New Delhi

Dilli Haat

An open-air crafts market with textiles, handmade goods, and food stalls from every Indian state. The best place in Delhi to pick up authentic gifts and keepsakes for Nairobi.

New Delhi · Crafts Market

Dilli Haat

A permanent open-air market where artisans from every Indian state sell their crafts directly. Textiles, pottery, jewellery, leather goods, and regional food stalls all in one place.

8:00 PM

Dinner

Will sort the restaurant nearer the time — somewhere worth the evening.

Day Six  ·  Wednesday 27 May 2026

Last Day in Delhi
Farewell and Departure

With

Neeresh · Smriti · Rajan · Pulkit

Morning — Free · Checkout by 12 Noon

Last Morning at Athiti

Pack up. A slow breakfast. One last walk around the garden. Five days go quickly.

9:30 AM — Central New Delhi

India Gate and Rajpath

The ceremonial heart of New Delhi — open, grand, and beautiful in the morning light. Good for photographs before the day gets going.

New Delhi · National Monument

India Gate

A 42-metre war memorial arch at the end of the ceremonial boulevard of New Delhi. The surrounding lawns and the grand sweep of Kartavya Path make it one of the most photogenic places in the city.

11:00 AM — Near India Gate

Cathedral Church of the Redemption

A striking colonial-era cathedral built in 1935, right near India Gate. One of Delhi's most beautiful and peaceful landmarks — white stucco, high arches, cool interior. Worth a quiet visit before the journey home.

1:00 PM — Greater Noida to IGI Airport

Pre-Booked Cab to the Airport

🚘

Cab departure — ~1h 15m drive

Target: airport by 2:15 PM · Plenty of time before 17:00 departure from Terminal 2

1:00 PM

17:00 — Delhi DEL T2 to Mumbai BOM T2

Flight to Mumbai, With Neeresh

Domestic, approximately 2 hours. Neeresh flies with Serah on this leg all the way through to Mumbai.

8:00 PM — ITC Maurya Hotel, New Delhi

Farewell Dinner — Bukhara

One of the most celebrated restaurants in Delhi. Heads of state have called it their favourite restaurant in India. The dal bukhara simmers for 18 hours. Must be booked well in advance.

◆

Farewell Dinner · ITC Maurya

Bukhara Restaurant

Neeresh · Smriti · Rajan · Pulkit — the whole group, one last time. The last proper evening all together before Serah heads home.

19:00–23:30 IST — Mumbai BOM T2

Mumbai Layover — Final Stop Before Nairobi

About 6 hours at the terminal. Check in for the Nairobi flight (ref T9CTRM). Have a meal. Rest. Mumbai T2 is comfortable for a long wait.

Day Seven  ·  Thursday 28 May 2026

Coming Home
Full Circle

With

01:55 IST — Mumbai BOM, Terminal 2

Flight 6E 1853 Departs — Mumbai to Nairobi

IndiGo · 6h 15m · Economy · 2 pieces of luggage (total max 25 kg) · Check-in ref T9CTRM. A night flight home — sleep well on board.

✈

Flight 6E 1853 · Mumbai BOM T2 to Nairobi NBO T1C

IndiGo · 6h 15m · Economy · 2 pieces luggage · Ref T9CTRM

01:55

05:40 AM — Nairobi Time

Lands at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport — Terminal 1C

Immigration. Baggage. Done. Serah is back in Nairobi before the day has properly started. Early morning, quiet airport, home ahead.

~7:00 AM — Nairobi

Transfer Home

Cab or pick-up arranged in advance. Full circle. Back home safely.

✦

She Is Well
Taken Care Of.

7

Days Away

4

Flight Legs

7+

People With Her

Serah is received at the airport the moment she lands in Delhi. Neeresh is there with her from arrival through to the flight back to Mumbai. At every flight connection, a familiar face is either waiting for her or travelling with her. She stays in a private, top-rated home with everything she needs. She is surrounded by family and close friends every day. And she lands back in Nairobi at 5:40 AM on Thursday 28 May at Terminal 1C — rested, safe, and with memories of India that will stay with her for a very long time.

This trip has been planned with full care — for her comfort, her safety, and her happiness.
